title: Exposure to nature is associated with decreased functional connectivity within the distress network: A resting state EEG study.

assets.abstract: INTRODUCTION: Despite the well-established evidence supporting the restorative potential of nature exposure, the neurophysiological underpinnings of the restorative cognitive/emotional effect of nature are not yet fully understood. The main purpose of the current study was to investigate the association between exposure to nature and electroencephalography (EEG) functional connectivity in the distress network. METHODS: Fifty-three individuals (11 men and 42 women; mean age 21.38 ± 1.54 years) we

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Nature connectedness — biophilia hypothesis, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience.
